_meta.provenance: Register of existing government systems referenced by the concept. System existence, ownership and purpose were verified against the cited sources on 2026-07-11. Proposed relationships remain KHELO BHARAT design proposals — none are approved integrations. apiConfirmed is true only where public API documentation was found (DigiLocker only).

systems.name: NSRS — National Sports Repository System
systems.owner: Ministry of Youth Affairs & Sports (MYAS) / Sports Authority of India (SAI)
systems.purpose: National registry of athletes, coaches, academies and support personnel with unique IDs.
systems.relationshipNote: Proposed as the identity backbone — every KHELO BHARAT record attaches to an NSRS ID.
facts: NSRS (nsrs.kheloindia.gov.in) is operated by SAI under MYAS as part of the Khelo India scheme; athletes, coaches and academies self-register.
On registration NSRS issues a unique Khelo India ID (KID) — the concept's term 'NSRS ID' must be reconciled with this (tracked as Q13).
An NSRS mobile app exists (active development) and DigiLocker integration has been announced as upcoming; no public API documentation found.

systems.name: PFMS — Public Financial Management System
systems.owner: Controller General of Accounts, Ministry of Finance
systems.purpose: Tracks central fund flows: sanction, release, utilisation; DBT payment rails.
systems.relationshipNote: Proposed mirror of sanction-to-utilisation status against scheme applications.
facts: PFMS (pfms.nic.in) is run by the Controller General of Accounts, Ministry of Finance; launched 2009, DBT-enabled since 2013.
Public 'Know Your Payment' tracking exposes payment stages: Validated → Approved → Payment Initiated → Success/Failed.

systems.name: eOffice
systems.owner: National Informatics Centre (NIC), MeitY; procedural owner DARPG (CSMOP)
systems.purpose: Government electronic file movement and workflow.
systems.relationshipNote: Proposed read-only mirror of file status for scheme applications.
facts: eOffice is a Mission Mode Project under the National e-Governance Plan, built by NIC; electronic files are authoritative per DARPG's amended CSMOP.
Since April 2020 all central ministries in New Delhi process files through eOffice.

systems.name: KIRTI — Khelo India Rising Talent Identification
systems.purpose: Talent assessment programme for school-age children.
systems.relationshipNote: Assessment results proposed to land on the Athlete 360 timeline.
facts: KIRTI launched 12 March 2024 in Chandigarh (Phase 2: 19 July 2024) for children aged 9–18; assessments run through notified Talent Assessment Centres.
Target of 20 lakh assessments/year across 11 disciplines; Phase 1 saw 3.6 lakh registrations across 70 centres and ~51,000 assessments in 28 states/UTs.

systems.name: DigiLocker
systems.purpose: Citizen document wallet with issued-document APIs.
systems.relationshipNote: Proposed for document submission and QR-verified certificate issuance.
facts: DigiLocker has publicly documented Issuer and Authorized-Partner APIs published through API Setu (MeitY/NeGD), with a formal partner-onboarding SOP.
This is the only system in this register with confirmed public API documentation — integration is technically credible, subject to onboarding approval.

systems.name: CPGRAMS — Centralised Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System
systems.relationshipNote: Sports grievances proposed to interoperate rather than duplicate.
facts: CPGRAMS (pgportal.gov.in) is run by DARPG and connects 92 central ministries/departments and 36 states/UTs with role-based access and nodal officers.
A sports grievance channel must interoperate with (not duplicate) this national system.

systems.name: NADA — National Anti-Doping Agency systems
systems.purpose: Anti-doping testing, results management, athlete whereabouts.
systems.relationshipNote: Doping flags proposed to follow NADA permissions strictly; no local copy.
facts: NADA India (nadaindia.yas.gov.in) is an autonomous body under MYAS; athlete whereabouts and testing records flow through WADA's ADAMS system, not a NADA-owned database.
Any doping-flag view in Khelo Bharat therefore depends on NADA/WADA permissions — stricter than a normal data-exchange.

systems.name: UDISE+ — Unified District Information System for Education
systems.purpose: School registry and education statistics; school codes.
systems.relationshipNote: School identity proposed to anchor screening-games capture.
facts: UDISE+ (udiseplus.gov.in, Ministry of Education) assigns every recognised school an 11-digit code (state-district-block-school) with public school lookup.
A stable national school identifier for the screening-games network is confirmed to exist.

systems.name: Fit India
systems.purpose: National fitness movement; school fitness assessments.
systems.relationshipNote: Screening games proposed to align with Fit India assessments to avoid duplicate testing.
facts: Fit India (fitindia.gov.in) is MYAS-nodal; the Fit India Mobile App (Aug 2021) runs fitness assessments on the age-appropriate 'GOALS' protocols (Sept 2020).
Schools already conduct annual fitness assessments and can register for Fit India certification — the screening-games test battery must align, not compete.

systems.name: PM GatiShakti National Master Plan (GIS)
systems.purpose: National GIS platform for infrastructure planning.
systems.relationshipNote: Facility layer proposed on national GIS base.
facts: PM GatiShakti NMP (launched 13 Oct 2021) is a BISAG-N-built GIS platform with 1,450+ data layers and ISRO imagery, spanning ministries' infrastructure.
A sports-facility layer is NOT confirmed to exist — placing one on this platform is a Khelo Bharat proposal requiring DPIIT/BISAG-N engagement.

systems.name: NPCI / RuPay
systems.relationshipNote: Proposed co-branded Khelo Bharat Sports Card with issuing banks.

sources.title: ICICI Bank partners with NPCI to launch RuPay credit cards

facts: Co-branded RuPay card programmes with issuing banks are an established NPCI model (e.g., ICICI Coral RuPay; PNB–Patanjali co-brand; SBI Card–Paytm).
Precedent confirms the Sports Card's co-branded model is commercially standard; a government-athlete co-brand still needs NPCI + issuing-bank agreements.

systems.name: DBT — Direct Benefit Transfer
systems.purpose: Direct transfer of scheme benefits to beneficiaries.
systems.relationshipNote: Scholarships/stipends proposed to land on the Sports Card via DBT rails.

sources.title: PFMS — single platform for payments and DBT

facts: DBT rails run through PFMS (confirmed via PFMS sources); the DBT Mission sits under the Cabinet Secretariat.
Direct confirmation from dbtbharat.gov.in pending — treated as partially verified.

systems.name: NCrF — National Credit Framework
systems.purpose: Credit framework spanning school, higher and vocational education.
systems.relationshipNote: Sport achievement-to-credit ladder proposed to map to NCrF levels. Requires MoE policy decision.
facts: NCrF was notified by UGC on 10 April 2023 and explicitly covers creditisation of sports, games and experiential learning across school, higher and vocational education.
Delhi schools have already granted credits for inter-school sports (50,000+ students in 2022-23) — a working precedent for Marks for Sports (M16).

systems.name: Aadhaar / APAAR
systems.purpose: Foundational identity; student lifetime academic ID (APAAR).
systems.relationshipNote: Identity de-duplication proposed via consented Aadhaar/APAAR linkage. Consent and DPDP review mandatory.
facts: APAAR ('One Nation One Student ID', apaar.education.gov.in) is a 12-digit lifetime academic ID; creation requires UDISE+ PEN and Aadhaar-matching name, with parental consent (withdrawable).
APAAR already stores co-curricular achievements — overlap and linkage with the athlete profile needs an explicit MoE conversation.

systems.name: MeitY-empanelled cloud hosting
systems.purpose: Empanelled cloud service providers for government workloads.
systems.relationshipNote: Hosting requirement, not a data integration.
facts: MeitY empanels cloud providers under the GI Cloud (MeghRaj) initiative with STQC audit, India data-residency and ISO 27001/17/18 compliance; procurement via GeM.
AWS, Azure, Google Cloud and multiple Indian providers are empanelled.

systems.name: DPDP Act 2023 — Digital Personal Data Protection Act
systems.purpose: Personal data protection law: consent, purpose limitation, children's data.
systems.relationshipNote: Compliance obligation across every module; consent vault and guardian consent are design responses.

sources.title: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (No. 22 of 2023)

facts: DPDP Act (No. 22 of 2023): Section 9(1) requires verifiable parent/guardian consent before processing personal data of anyone under 18.
Draft DPDP Rules 2025 phase in fiduciary obligations (including children's-data duties) over ~18 months — the consent-vault design must track the final rules.

systems.name: National Sports Governance Act 2025 (National Sports Board, National Sports Tribunal)
systems.purpose: Governance framework for federations: recognition, elections, tribunals, athlete commissions.
systems.relationshipNote: M2 is proposed as the Act's digital compliance backbone. Statutory details require verification against the gazette text.

sources.title: The National Sports Governance Act, 2025 (Act No. 25 of 2025)
The National Sports Governance Bill, 2025 — bill track

facts: National Sports Governance Act, 2025 (Act No. 25 of 2025) received Presidential assent 18 Aug 2025; select provisions in force from 31 Dec 2025 (S.O. 6152(E), Gazette 1 Jan 2026).
Creates the National Sports Board (recognition/regulation of sports bodies) and the National Sports Tribunal (SC-judge-led); draft Tribunal Rules published by MYAS.
M2's compliance backbone maps directly onto this statutory framework.

systems.name: Khelo India / TOPS / NCOE / STC programmes
systems.purpose: Flagship schemes: Khelo India games & scholarships, Target Olympic Podium Scheme, National Centres of Excellence, SAI Training Centres.
systems.relationshipNote: Scheme and centre data proposed to consolidate into registries and Athlete 360.

sources.title: Khelo India — National Programme for Development of Sports (factsheet)

facts: Khelo India is MYAS's flagship Central Sector Scheme (merger of RGKA, USIS and NSTSS).
TOPS has run since September 2014 with the Mission Olympic Cell (April 2016) and ₹50,000/month out-of-pocket allowance for core athletes; NCOEs operate under SAI.
